skillName: Position negatives on a number line
misconceptionSummary: Students count negative intervals in the wrong direction, placing -3 to the right of -1.

steps.narration: Zero sits at the centre. Positive numbers extend to the right; negative numbers extend to the left. Each step left subtracts one from the value.
Start at zero and count four steps to the left. Each step takes you one further into the negatives. Four steps left lands you at negative four.
Mark the point with a dot and label it with the negative sign included. The sign is part of the number — leaving it off changes the meaning entirely.

visuals.label: Start at 0 → count left → mark and label with − sign

steps.narration: Negative four. Start at zero, count left, and always include the negative sign in the label.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: counting steps to the right when placing a negative number. Negatives always go LEFT of zero — the larger the digit, the further left.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Negative numbers live to the left of zero. Count left from zero, not right. The digit tells you how many steps — the sign tells you which direction.
